banner.title: Fourteen years of cooking the way we always wanted to.
banner.subtitle: An intimate atelier built on quiet sourcing, slow technique, and the conviction that restraint is the most generous thing a kitchen can offer.

philosophy.title: A few principles we never break.
philosophy.subtitle: Worn into the kitchen over years, kept simple on purpose.

items.title: Sourcing first
items.description: If we cannot name the grower, we do not serve it. The menu is written the morning the deliveries arrive.

items.title: Restraint over reach
items.description: Three or four elements per plate. Always. Anything more is a distraction we have not earned.

items.title: Quiet hospitality
items.description: Service should anticipate without intruding. The room disappears; the meal remains.

items.title: Seasons over scale
items.description: Twelve seats, two seatings. We grow inward, not outward — better, not bigger.

process.title: How a night at Maison Royale unfolds.
process.subtitle: The arc of the meal is composed as carefully as the dishes themselves.

steps.title: Welcome at the door
steps.description: A glass of something effervescent in the parlour while the room finishes setting.

steps.title: Seven small openings
steps.description: A succession of bright, considered bites — the prologue to the evening.

steps.title: Hearth-led plates
steps.description: The kitchen moves to the open fire. Larger, more contemplative courses arrive in rhythm.

steps.title: A quiet finale
steps.description: Pastry, mignardises, a final pour. We never rush this part of the evening.

team.title: A very small team. A very long bench.
team.subtitle: Three of the faces you will meet behind the pass.

members.name: Elena Voss
members.bio: Returned from Lyon and San Sebastián to open the room she always wanted to cook in.

members.name: Mateo Rivera
members.bio: Eleven years in low-intervention cellars across the Loire, the Jura, and the Willamette.

members.name: Iris Tanaka
members.bio: Builds the final act of every menu — quieter than savoury, never less considered.
